verses.1: A prayer of Habakkuk, the prophet, set to victorious music.
verses.2: LORD, I have heard of your fame.
verses.4: His splendor is like the sunrise.
verses.6: He stood, and shook the earth.
verses.7: I saw the tents of Cushan in affliction.
verses.8: Was the LORD displeased with the rivers?
verses.10: The mountains saw you, and were afraid.
verses.11: The sun and moon stood still in the sky
verses.12: You marched through the land in wrath.
verses.13: You went out for the salvation of your people,
verses.14: You pierced the heads of his warriors with their own spears.
verses.15: You trampled the sea with your horses,
verses.16: I heard, and my body trembled.
verses.17: For even though the fig tree doesn’t flourish,
verses.18: yet I will rejoice in the LORD.
verses.19: GOD, the Lord, is my strength.
